systemName: Wing⚡Boss Staff Academy Expanded SOP System
academyRules: Lessons must stay short enough for service-time review.
English is the source of truth.
Khmer text must be reviewed before live staff use.
Do not start cooking if required order information is missing.
Do not invent prices when PRICE_REQUIRED_FROM_MENU appears.
Heat Points are earned only after paid, completed, not refunded orders.

lessons.title: Read the 3-step order first
lessons.english: Every direct order must be read in this order: item, flavor, then add-ons. Do not start cooking until those three parts are clear.
lessons.staffAction: Check item, quantity, flavor, heat, and add-ons before cooking.
lessons.commonMistake: Starting the fryer before checking the flavor.
lessons.quickCheckQuestion: What are the first three parts to check?

lessons.title: Check quantity and serving units
lessons.english: Wing counts affect flavor limits, serving units, sauce amount, rub amount, and drizzle amount. Always check the count before preparing sauce or rub.
lessons.staffAction: Confirm wing count before portioning sauce, rub, or drizzle.
lessons.commonMistake: Using a 6-wing sauce portion for a 12-wing order.
lessons.quickCheckQuestion: Why do we check quantity first?
lessons.quickCheckAnswer: Because quantity controls portions and flavor limits.

lessons.title: Advanced flavor numbers matter
lessons.english: Advanced customers may order by number. The number is part of the order and helps staff avoid confusing similar flavor names.
lessons.staffAction: Use the number and name together when reading the ticket.
lessons.commonMistake: Reading only the name and missing the formula.
lessons.quickCheckQuestion: What should staff read for advanced flavors?
lessons.quickCheckAnswer: Both the number and the flavor name.

lessons.title: Sauce and dry rub are not the same
lessons.english: Sauce means wet coating. Dry rub means seasoning powder. Do not swap them.
lessons.staffAction: Confirm whether the selected flavor is sauce or dry rub before tossing.
lessons.commonMistake: Adding wet sauce when the ticket says dry rub.
lessons.quickCheckAnswer: Sauce is wet coating; dry rub is seasoning powder.

lessons.title: Dry rub marker must be visible
lessons.english: Dry rub items must be clearly marked on staff tickets so the kitchen does not treat them like sauces.
lessons.staffAction: Look for dry-rub type and staff label before prep.
lessons.commonMistake: Missing the dry-rub label and using sauce.
lessons.quickCheckQuestion: What marker should staff look for?

lessons.title: Default heat is no added spice
lessons.english: If the customer does not choose a heat tier, use the default heat only. Do not add heat automatically.
lessons.staffAction: Prepare the flavor as ordered without extra heat.
lessons.commonMistake: Adding hot sauce because the customer likes spicy food.

lessons.title: Heat upgrades must show clearly
lessons.english: Heat modifiers affect the customer total, staff warning, and heat score. Staff must see the selected heat tier clearly.
lessons.staffAction: Confirm heat tier before cooking and before packing.
lessons.commonMistake: Preparing Original heat when the ticket says Revenge.

lessons.title: Nuclear is restricted
lessons.english: Nuclear must only be used when the active menu rules allow it. If unsure, ask owner before cooking.
lessons.staffAction: Do not improvise Nuclear. Confirm availability and allowed base.
lessons.commonMistake: Giving Nuclear on a regular dry rub or normal flavor without approval.
lessons.quickCheckQuestion: What should staff do if Nuclear is unclear?
lessons.quickCheckAnswer: Ask owner or manager before cooking.

lessons.title: Add-ons are not free unless included
lessons.english: Do not give extra sauce, rub, drizzle, dip, cheese, bottle, or heat upgrade unless selected and charged or explicitly included by the menu.
lessons.commonMistake: Adding a free dip because the customer usually asks for one.
lessons.quickCheckQuestion: When can staff include an add-on?
lessons.quickCheckAnswer: Only when selected, charged, or explicitly included.

lessons.title: Drizzle goes last
lessons.english: Sauce first, dry rub second, drizzle last. This preserves flavor clarity and makes the ticket easier to execute.
lessons.exampleOrder: Wings + dry rub + Hot Honey drizzle
lessons.quickCheckQuestion: What is the correct prep order?
lessons.quickCheckAnswer: Sauce first, dry rub second, drizzle last.

lessons.title: Use a final pack check
lessons.english: Before handoff, check item, flavor, add-ons, dips, drink, napkins, and customer name.
lessons.staffAction: Use the packing checklist before giving order to customer or driver.
lessons.quickCheckQuestion: What must be checked before handoff?
lessons.quickCheckAnswer: Item, flavor, add-ons, dips, drink, napkins, customer name.

lessons.title: Separate hot sauce bottles from hot food
lessons.english: Bottled hot sauce should be packed safely and visibly, not buried under hot food where it can be missed or damaged.
lessons.staffAction: Pack bottle separately or visibly in the bag.
lessons.commonMistake: Putting bottle under fries or wings.

lessons.title: Confirm missing information before cooking
lessons.english: If phone, address, flavor, item, or payment proof is missing, do not start cooking. Ask the customer first.
lessons.staffAction: Send the short confirmation script and wait for reply.
lessons.quickCheckQuestion: What do we do when key info is missing?
lessons.quickCheckAnswer: Ask first; do not cook yet.

lessons.title: Missing price means staff confirms total
lessons.english: If the app says Staff will confirm, staff must confirm total before final payment/cooking. Do not invent price.
lessons.staffAction: Escalate to owner or use current printed menu before confirming.
lessons.quickCheckAnswer: Do not guess; staff or owner must confirm.

lessons.title: Out of stock can come back later
lessons.english: Unavailable does not mean deleted. Offer a replacement and keep the customer conversation simple.
lessons.staffAction: Say it is unavailable today and offer closest replacement.
lessons.commonMistake: Telling customer it is gone forever.
lessons.quickCheckQuestion: What should staff say for unavailable items?

lessons.title: Pay first before cooking
lessons.english: For bank transfer orders, staff should wait for payment proof before cooking or dispatching to kitchen.
lessons.staffAction: Ask for payment proof photo before staff dispatch.
lessons.commonMistake: Cooking before payment proof when pay-first rule applies.
lessons.quickCheckQuestion: When can staff dispatch bank-transfer orders?

lessons.title: Direct order is about clarity, not arguing with apps
lessons.english: Use direct-order language that sounds helpful: faster reorder, clearer customization, staff confirmation. Do not insult delivery apps to customers.
lessons.staffAction: Explain direct order is faster for repeat orders and easier for customizations.
lessons.commonMistake: Saying delivery apps are bad or blaming the customer.
lessons.quickCheckQuestion: What is the safe direct-order message?
lessons.quickCheckAnswer: Faster reorder, clearer customization, staff confirms directly.

lessons.title: Clean and reset after rush
lessons.english: After a rush, reset sauce area, dry rub area, packaging area, and order screen before next order.
lessons.staffAction: Clean spills, close containers, check low stock, and reset tools.
lessons.commonMistake: Leaving sauce lids open and confusing rub spoons.
lessons.quickCheckAnswer: Sauce, dry rub, packaging, and order screen.

orderReadingExamples.title: Standard sauce order
orderReadingExamples.staffRisk: Do not forget paid dip cup.

orderReadingExamples.title: Missing price order
orderReadingExamples.staffRisk: Wrong price damages trust and margin.

orderReadingExamples.title: Bottle add-on
orderReadingExamples.staffRisk: Bottle can be missed if packed under food.

orderReadingExamples.title: Delivery order
orderReadingExamples.staffRisk: Do not send driver without address.
